name: SKILL.md
content:
---
name: datamerge
description: >-
  Illustrator Data Merge — fill one Adobe Illustrator template from a list of records and get one finished .ai file per record. It is the Illustrator version of InDesign's Data Merge: point it at a template plus data (a spreadsheet, CSV, list, spec-sheet PDFs, or typed values) and it stamps out a populated file per row, swapping text, numbers, and linked images while preserving every font, color, and the layout. Use it whenever someone needs the "same layout, different content, many versions" job: back labels per SKU, shelf tags and price cards, hang tags, name badges and ID cards, certificates, business cards from a roster, regional variants of one layout, spec sheets, and Nutrition, Supplement, or Drug Facts panels. Trigger on "make one for each", "a version per SKU/flavor/person", "populate this template from this list", "batch out these labels", "merge this data into the artwork", or any template-plus-data job. Works for any client and any template, even if they never say "data merge".
---

# Illustrator Data Merge

> **v1.0.1** — Fill an Adobe Illustrator template from data: one finished file per record.

Illustrator Data Merge does for Illustrator what Data Merge does for InDesign. You give it one
**template** (an `.ai` file laid out once) and a set of **records** (rows of data), and it
produces one populated, print-ready `.ai` per record — swapping the text, numbers, and linked
images you choose, while leaving fonts, color, type styling, and layout untouched. It is the
build sibling to ColorProof: ColorProof corrects color on finished art; Data Merge generates the
files in the first place.

It is **client- and template-agnostic.** Nothing about any one job is baked into the engine. A
new template is *profiled* once (which text frame holds the price, which image is the photo);
that profile is saved and reused. A new dataset is just new records. Nutrition Facts is one
built-in domain pack, not the whole tool.

## What people ask for (you don't need magic words)

Real requests this handles — recognize the shape, then run the workflow below:

- "Make a back label for each of these 14 wines, same layout, just change the varietal, vintage,
  and ABV."
- "Here's a spreadsheet of 200 employees — build a name badge for each with their name, title,
  and headshot."
- "Generate a shelf tag per SKU from this price list."
- "We need this certificate for everyone on the attached list."
- "Populate the Nutrition Facts panel for every flavor from these spec sheets."
- "Same poster, but a version for each city with its date and venue."

If the job is *one layout, many versions, driven by a list,* it's a data merge.

## Safety mental model

The mechanical work is done by a vetted Illustrator engine (`scripts/datamerge.jsx`), not by
improvising in chat. Your job is orchestration: profile the template, gather and check the
records, assemble the job, run the engine, read its JSON result, and verify. The engine makes
**content edits only** — it swaps text in place (preserving each character's font) and relinks
named images (refitting them into the same box). It never changes color, type styling, position,
or layout. Files are regenerated from the template each run, and the output folder is
snapshotted before any write.

## Where it lives (deployed on the Mac)

Engine and helpers install at `~/.datamerge/`:

- `datamerge.jsx` — the ES3-safe engine. Reads `~/.datamerge/job.jsxinc` (global `DATAMERGE_JOB`)
  and writes `~/.datamerge/result.json`.
- `run.sh` — runner. Runs the engine in Illustrator **wrapped in `with timeout of 1800 seconds`**
  (macOS otherwise caps a `do javascript` event at ~120s), then builds + opens the HTML report.
- `report.js` — Node generator: `result.json` → self-contained dark `report.html`.
- `snapshot.sh` — backs up the output folder before any edit.
- `profiles/` — saved per-template field maps, built once and reused.

The skill's `scripts/` folder holds canonical copies; if `~/.datamerge/` is missing or stale,
redeploy from there (see `references/install.md`).

## The workflow (four phases)

```
1. PROFILE the template  (once per template)  → mode "discover"  → references/template-profiles.md
2. GATHER + check the records                  → per record       → references/use-cases.md (+ domain packs)
3. BUILD  (copy + populate)                     → mode "build"     → references/job-schema.md
4. VERIFY  (snapshot values + render proofs)    → read result.json + look at the PNGs
```

### Phase 1 — Profile the template (once)

A profile maps each variable field to the text frame (or named image) that holds it, and the
edit method. Frame indices are specific to a template, so discover them — never hardcode.

1. Write a discover job: `var DATAMERGE_JOB = { mode:"discover", template:"/abs/Template.ai" };`
2. Run it (Illustrator open). The report lists every text frame (index, contents, position,
   **font runs**) and every **named linked image**.
3. Build the field map. Font runs pick the method: single-font frame → a safe text swap;
   two-font frame (bold label + value, or "INGREDIENTS:" + body) → `bodyAfter` to protect the
   label's font; a named linked image → `image`.
4. Save it to `~/.datamerge/profiles/<template-name>.jsxinc` and reuse it forever.

Heuristics, multi-panel templates, naming images: **`references/template-profiles.md`**.

### Phase 2 — Gather and check the records

Turn the source (spreadsheet/CSV, list, spec-sheet PDFs, typed values) into one record per
output, each a set of `key → value` plus any per-record image paths. **Show the user the
assembled table before building** — for anything regulated or client-facing, that eyeball is the
cheapest error check there is. Domain specifics (e.g. the FDA field set, rounding, %DV) live in
the domain packs; the general use-case catalog and how to phrase each is in
**`references/use-cases.md`**.

### Phase 3 — Build

Write a build job (template, output folder, the saved profile, optional one-time template fixes,
and the records) and run it. The engine copies the template per record, populates the mapped
fields style-safely, saves, and records a verification snapshot. Large batch → background it and
poll `result.json`:

```bash
rm -f ~/.datamerge/result.json
nohup zsh ~/.datamerge/run.sh >/tmp/dm.log 2>&1 & disown
```

Job/result schema and the five edit methods: **`references/job-schema.md`**.

### Phase 4 — Verify

The text snapshot proves values landed; it can't prove fonts survived or images look right.
Always **render** representative outputs (set `render:true`; the report embeds thumbnails) and
look at them. Illustrator's PNG export is **sandboxed out of `/tmp`** — render into a folder under
the output directory (the engine defaults to `<outputDir>/_previews`, proven writable). Treat
`_previews` as scratch; delete before final handoff.

## Edit methods (in a profile field)

- `sub` — swap a text substring in place (numbers, %, a word). Single-font frames and numbers
  inside single-font combined frames.
- `bodyAfter` — replace everything after a prefix, keeping the prefix's font (mixed-font
  label:value frames, ingredient lines).
- `removeSub` — delete a substring (template cleanups).
- `contents` — replace a whole single-font frame.
- `image` — relink a **named linked** placed image to a per-record file and refit it into the
  same box (`preserveAspect:true` to letterbox instead of fill).

## Operating principles

- **Source is the source of truth.** Transcribe exactly; reproduce oddities and flag them rather
  than silently "fixing." Don't recompute values the data already gives.
- **Verify before write, snapshot before batch.** A field whose expected value isn't found is
  flagged (`NOTFOUND`), never guessed. Revert is a file copy.
- **Content-only, style-preserving.** Fonts, color, type styling, and layout never change.
- **Never global-replace.** Target by the profile's frame index / image name, not a blind
  find-and-replace (templates reuse strings and may carry decoy elements).
- **Copy-first on new ground.** Build ONE record first, render it, confirm layout and fonts,
  then batch.

## Honest limitations

- Fills an existing template; it does not lay one out or create/move objects.
- Images must be **linked** placed items with a name in the Layers panel (embedded images can't
  be relinked). It refits into the existing box; it doesn't crop intelligently.
- One layout/format per profile; a different panel on the same file is a second profile.
- Illustrator must be open (no headless mode); template fonts must be installed.
- PNG proofs export to the output folder, not `/tmp`.

## First-run setup

First time on a machine → **`references/install.md`** (engine deployed, Illustrator open,
folder access, automation permission). After that it's pure chat. A friendly step-by-step for
non-technical teammates ships as `datamerge-install-guide.html`.

name: references/domain-nutrition-facts.md
content:
# Domain pack — Nutrition / Supplement / Drug Facts

A domain pack is reusable knowledge for one kind of content. This one covers FDA facts panels:
the canonical field keys, the %DV reference values (to compute missing %DV), rounding rules, and
the fidelity rules that cause real-world bugs. Everything else (the engine, profiling, building)
is the general tool.

## Canonical field keys (Nutrition Facts)

| key | meaning | example | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `cal` | Calories | `110` | whole number |
| `totfat` / `totfat_dv` | Total Fat / %DV | `9g` / `12%` | include the unit |
| `satfat` / `satfat_dv` | Saturated Fat / %DV | `5g` / `25%` | |
| `trans` | Trans Fat | `0g` | no %DV |
| `chol` / `chol_dv` | Cholesterol / %DV | `30mg` / `10%` | |
| `sodium` / `sodium_dv` | Sodium / %DV | `190mg` / `8%` | |
| `carb` / `carb_dv` | Total Carbohydrate / %DV | `1g` / `0%` | |
| `fiber` / `fiber_dv` | Dietary Fiber / %DV | `0g` / `0%` | |
| `sugars` | Total Sugars | `0g` | no %DV |
| `addsugars` / `addsugars_dv` | Added Sugars / %DV | `0g` / `0%` | |
| `protein` | Protein | `6g` | %DV usually omitted on conventional food |
| `vitd` / `vitd_dv` | Vitamin D / %DV | `0mcg` / `0%` | |
| `calcium` / `calcium_dv` | Calcium / %DV | `201mg` / `15%` | |
| `iron` / `iron_dv` | Iron / %DV | `0mg` / `0%` | |
| `potassium` / `potassium_dv` | Potassium / %DV | `22mg` / `0%` | |
| `serving` | Serving size | `1 oz (28g)` | often constant |
| `servings` | Servings/container | `Varies by pkg servings per container` | |
| `ingredients` | Ingredient body | ` PASTEURIZED MILK, ...` | usually `bodyAfter` |
| `allergens` | Allergen/Contains | `milk` | often a constant frame; may be unmapped |

Map only the keys the template exposes. Supplement Facts and Drug Facts use the same machinery
with their own headings and field sets ("Amount Per Serving", "% Daily Value", "Other
Ingredients"; "Active ingredient", "Purpose", "Uses", "Warnings").

## %DV reference values (adults & children ≥4)

Compute only when a source omits %DV: `%DV = round(amount / DV * 100)`. Prefer the source's
printed %DV when given.

| nutrient | DV | nutrient | DV |
|---|---|---|---|
| Total Fat | 78 g | Added Sugars | 50 g |
| Saturated Fat | 20 g | Protein | 50 g |
| Cholesterol | 300 mg | Vitamin D | 20 mcg |
| Sodium | 2300 mg | Calcium | 1300 mg |
| Total Carbohydrate | 275 g | Iron | 18 mg |
| Dietary Fiber | 28 g | Potassium | 4700 mg |

## FDA rounding (summary)

- **Calories:** ≤50 → nearest 5; >50 → nearest 10.
- **Fat:** <0.5 g → `0g`; 0.5–5 g → nearest 0.5 g; >5 g → nearest 1 g.
- **Cholesterol:** <2 mg → `0mg`; 2–5 mg → "less than 5 mg"; >5 mg → nearest 5 mg.
- **Sodium / Potassium:** <5 mg → `0mg`; 5–140 → nearest 5; >140 → nearest 10.
- **Carb / Fiber / Sugars / Protein:** <0.5 g → `0g`; <1 g → "less than 1 g"; ≥1 g → nearest 1 g.
- **Vit/min:** %DV to nearest 1%.

Most supplier spec sheets are already rounded — reproduce as printed; compute only from raw data.

## Fidelity rules (the real bugs)

- **Ingredients: reproduce exactly.** Casing, commas, terminal punctuation. If a sheet ends a
  line without a period while siblings have one, keep it and **flag it** — don't silently fix.
- **Colored vs white variants.** "ANNATTO VEGETABLE COLOR (IF COLORED)" can cover a colored and
  a white SKU with one identical panel; a truly white product may instead drop the colorant
  ("...ENZYMES."). Read the sheet — don't assume.
- **Added ingredients change the line.** A "with jalapeño peppers" SKU lists peppers and may
  carry different nutrition; never clone a sibling's line.
- **Allergens / Contains** must stay consistent with the ingredient list. If identical across
  all products, leave that frame unmapped (constant).
- **Identical numbers still get their own file** unless told otherwise.

## Worked profile

See `assets/example-profile-nutrition.jsxinc` — the Dinner Bell vertical Nutrition Facts panel,
a complete field map (16 value fields via `sub` + ingredients via `bodyAfter`), which produced
11 product files in the original job.

name: references/install.md
content:
# Install / first-run setup

Illustrator Data Merge runs a small scripting engine from `~/.datamerge/` and drives it with a
shell runner. One-time setup per machine. A friendly, non-technical version of these steps ships
as `datamerge-install-guide.html` — hand that to teammates.

## 1. Deploy the engine

The skill's `scripts/` folder holds canonical copies. Deploy (or redeploy if stale):

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.datamerge/profiles ~/.datamerge/dist
cp scripts/datamerge.jsx scripts/run.sh scripts/snapshot.sh scripts/report.js ~/.datamerge/
chmod +x ~/.datamerge/run.sh ~/.datamerge/snapshot.sh
```

Requires Adobe Illustrator, Node (for the report), and zsh (default on macOS).

## 2. Illustrator must be open

No headless mode. The runner wraps the call in `with timeout of 1800 seconds` so long batches
don't hit the ~120s Apple-event cap.

## 3. Automation permission

The first run triggers macOS Automation permission ("… wants to control Adobe Illustrator").
Approve it. If a job returns nothing, check System Settings → Privacy & Security → Automation.

## 4. Folder access (Cowork / Claude Code)

Grant access to the job folders — the template, the output directory, the source data, and any
per-record image folders.

## 5. The /tmp export gotcha

Illustrator's PNG export writes **nothing** to `/tmp` (no error). Render into a folder under the
output directory; the engine defaults to `<outputDir>/_previews`, proven writable.

## 6. Smoke test

```js
// ~/.datamerge/job.jsxinc
var DATAMERGE_JOB = { mode:"discover", template:"/abs/path/AnyTemplate.ai" };
```

```bash
zsh ~/.datamerge/run.sh
```

A `report.html` listing the template's text frames (and any named images) means the engine,
runner, permission, and report generator all work.

## Where things live

| path | what |
|------|------|
| `~/.datamerge/datamerge.jsx` | the engine |
| `~/.datamerge/run.sh` | timeout-wrapped runner |
| `~/.datamerge/snapshot.sh` | folder backup before edits |
| `~/.datamerge/report.js` | result.json → report.html |
| `~/.datamerge/job.jsxinc` | current job (you write this) |
| `~/.datamerge/result.json` | last result (engine writes) |
| `~/.datamerge/report.html` | last report (auto-opened) |
| `~/.datamerge/profiles/` | saved per-template field maps |

name: references/job-schema.md
content:
# Job & result schema

The engine reads `~/.datamerge/job.jsxinc` (defining the global `DATAMERGE_JOB`, plain
evaluable JS — no JSON parser needed on the Mac) and writes `~/.datamerge/result.json`.

## Modes

- **`discover`** — dump a template's text frames + named linked images. Input: `template`.
- **`build`** — optional `masterFixes`, then copy + populate each record. Inputs: `template`,
  `outputDir`, `profile`, `records` (alias `products`), optional `masterFixes`, `render`.
- **`render`** — re-export PNG previews for listed records.

## Edit methods (in `profile.fields[]` and `masterFixes[]`)

- **`sub`** — `{ method:"sub", from:"<old>", frame:N }`. Style-safe substring swap → value.
- **`removeSub`** — `{ method:"removeSub", sub:"<text>", frame:N }`. Delete a substring (runs
  even with no record value).
- **`bodyAfter`** — `{ method:"bodyAfter", prefix:"INGREDIENTS:", frame:N }`. Replace everything
  after `prefix`, preserving the prefix's font.
- **`contents`** — `{ method:"contents", frame:N }`. Replace a whole single-font frame.
- **`image`** — `{ method:"image", name:"photo" }` (or `index:N`). Relink a named linked image to
  the record's file path and refit into the same box. Add `preserveAspect:true` to letterbox
  instead of fill; `fit:false` to keep the new image's native size.

`from` matches the **current** frame contents. Because every output is copied fresh from the
(optionally fixed) master each run, `from` is the template/master placeholder, constant across
records. A miss returns `NOTFOUND` (text) or `NOIMG` / `FILEMISSING` (image) in flags, never a
guess.

## Build job — example (mixed text + image)

```js
var DATAMERGE_JOB = {
  mode: "build",
  template: "/abs/Name Badge.ai",
  outputDir: "/abs/Badges Output",
  renderSubdir: "_previews",   // under outputDir; writable; scratch (delete before handoff)
  render: true,
  renderScale: 200,            // export percent
  freshCopy: true,             // copy template -> each output every run (idempotent). default true

  masterFixes: [               // optional, applied once to the template before copying
    { frame:5, method:"removeSub", sub:" (DRAFT)" }
  ],

  profile: {                   // paste a saved profile's fields array
    fields: [
      { key:"name",  frame:0, method:"sub", from:"FULL NAME" },
      { key:"title", frame:1, method:"sub", from:"TITLE" },
      { key:"photo", method:"image", name:"headshot", preserveAspect:true }
    ]
  },

  records: [
    { file:"Badge - A Rivera.ai", values:{ name:"Ana Rivera", title:"Designer",
        photo:"/abs/headshots/arivera.jpg" } },
    { file:"Badge - J Okafor.ai", values:{ name:"Jide Okafor", title:"Strategist",
        photo:"/abs/headshots/jokafor.jpg" } }
  ]
};
```

Notes:
- `values` keys must match the profile field `key`s. A field with no matching value is skipped,
  so constant fields can be left out.
- `records` and `products` are interchangeable (older jobs used `products`).
- To emit two named files from identical data, add two records with the same `values`.
- A `bodyAfter` body usually starts with a leading space so it reads `PREFIX value`.

## Result schema

```js
{ ok:true, mode:"build", ts:"...", template:"...", outputDir:"...",
  masterFixes:[ { frame, method, result } ],
  products:[
    { file:"Badge - A Rivera.ai", status:"OK"|"FLAGS"|"ERROR",
      flags:[ "photo:NOIMG" ],
      snapshot:{ name:"Ana Rivera", title:"Designer", photo:"[image] /abs/headshots/arivera.jpg" },
      render:{ png:"/abs/_previews/Badge___A_Rivera.png", exists:true } } ]
}
```

`result.json` is rewritten after **each** record, so a long batch shows progress and a crash
never loses finished work. `report.js` renders it to `report.html` with snapshot tables and
embedded thumbnails.

## Running

```bash
zsh ~/.datamerge/run.sh                                   # small job

rm -f ~/.datamerge/result.json                            # large batch: background + poll
nohup zsh ~/.datamerge/run.sh >/tmp/dm.log 2>&1 & disown
```

Always `snapshot.sh "<outputDir>"` before a build that overwrites existing outputs.

name: references/template-profiles.md
content:
# Template profiles

A **profile** maps each variable field to the Illustrator text frame (or named linked image)
that holds it, plus the edit method. Built once per template, reused on every job that uses it.
Frame indices are specific to a template's construction — discover them, never assume.

## 1. Run discover

```js
// ~/.datamerge/job.jsxinc
var DATAMERGE_JOB = { mode:"discover", template:"/abs/path/Template.ai" };
```

Run `zsh ~/.datamerge/run.sh`. The report (and `result.json`) lists every text frame and every
linked image:

```
{ "i":64, "contents":"$0.00", "x":340, "y":120, "runs":[{"font":"Helvetica-Bold","text":"$0.00"}] }
{ "i":12, "contents":"VARIETAL", "x":40, "y":300, "runs":[{"font":"Archivo-Black","text":"VARIETAL"}] }
images: [ { "index":0, "name":"photo", "file":"...placeholder.png" } ]
```

## 2. Read the font runs — they pick the method

- **One run (single font)** → `sub`. Safe substring swap; the whole frame is one style. Covers
  plain value frames and single-font "label value" frames (swap only the number/word).
- **Two or more runs (mixed fonts)** → `bodyAfter`. A bold label + regular value, or
  "INGREDIENTS:" + body. Keeps the prefix's font, rewrites only the body.
- **A named linked image** → `image`. Map by the name shown in the discover image list.
- **`contents`** (replace whole frame) only for a confirmed single-font frame; prefer `sub`.

## 3. Name your images in the template (one-time, in Illustrator)

For per-record photos/logos, the placed image must be **linked** (not embedded) and have a
**name** in the Layers panel. In Illustrator: select the placed image → double-click its row in
the Layers panel → type a name (e.g. `photo`, `logo`, `signature`). Discover then shows that
name; map it with `{ key:"photo", method:"image", name:"photo" }`. Embedded images can't be
relinked — if discover shows an image with an empty name or it's embedded, name/relink it once.

## 4. Map the fields

For each variable field record `{ key, frame, method, from }` (text) or
`{ key, method:"image", name }` (image). `from` is the template's current placeholder for that
frame; pick a unique token (the existing number/word). The engine swaps `from` → the record's
value. Frames that never change (a fixed legal line, a header) stay unmapped.

## 5. Multi-element templates and decoys

A template may hold more than one layout, or leftover placeholder elements. Discover shows them
all; cluster by position (`x,y`) to identify the layout you're populating, and map only its
frames. Two layouts that must both populate = two profiles (or two field sets), run as two jobs.

## 6. Save and reuse

Save to `~/.datamerge/profiles/<template-name>.jsxinc`:

```js
var PROFILE_wine_backlabel = {
  template: "Wine Back Label.ai",
  fields: [
    { key:"varietal", frame:12, method:"sub", from:"VARIETAL" },
    { key:"vintage",  frame:13, method:"sub", from:"2020" },
    { key:"abv",      frame:21, method:"sub", from:"0.0%" },
    { key:"photo",    method:"image", name:"bottleshot", preserveAspect:true }
  ]
};
```

Paste the `fields` array into the build job's `profile`. Worked examples ship in
`assets/example-profile-nutrition.jsxinc` and `assets/example-profile-badge.jsxinc`.

## Validating a new profile

Always build ONE record first with `render:true`, open the PNG, confirm values and fonts (and
that images land in the right box), then batch. A `NOTFOUND` flag means the `from` placeholder
didn't match — re-check the discover dump for that frame's exact contents (watch leading spaces).
A `NOIMG` flag means no linked image with that name was found; name it in the Layers panel.

name: references/use-cases.md
content:
# Use cases

Illustrator Data Merge fits any "one layout, many versions, driven by a list" job. This catalog
maps common Capsule work to the fields and methods you'd use. The pattern is always the same:
profile the template once, turn the source into records, build, verify.

## Packaging & product

- **Back labels per SKU/flavor/variety** — text (`sub`) for product name, descriptors, net
  weight, ABV, lot; `bodyAfter` for ingredient lines; `image` for a per-flavor bottle shot or
  icon. Source: a product spreadsheet or supplier sheets.
- **Nutrition / Supplement / Drug Facts panels** — see `references/domain-nutrition-facts.md`
  for the FDA field set, rounding, and %DV. The original worked example.
- **Spec sheets / cut sheets, one per product** — text fields + a product `image`.
- **Variant call-outs** (e.g. "Caffeine Free", "New Recipe") — `sub` a banner string.

## Retail & signage

- **Shelf tags / price cards** — product, price (`sub`), SKU/barcode area; optional `image` for
  the product. Source: a price list CSV.
- **Posters/flyers per location** — city, date, venue, price via `sub`; venue/sponsor `image`.
- **Table tents, danglers, header cards** — same idea, different template.

## People & events

- **Name badges / ID cards** — name, title, company (`sub`) + `image` headshot. Source: an
  attendee or employee roster. (`preserveAspect:true` on the headshot so it letterboxes cleanly.)
- **Certificates & awards** — recipient, date, program (`sub`); optional `image` signature.
- **Business cards from a roster** — name, title, phone, email (`sub`).

## Brand & localization

- **Multilingual / regional variants** — swap each text block per locale with `bodyAfter` or
  `sub`; keep layout identical. One record per language.
- **Co-branded versions** — partner name (`sub`) + partner `logo` (`image`).
- **Templated social/ad sizes with changing copy** — headline/subhead/CTA via `sub`.

## How to phrase the data

Whatever the source, reduce it to **one record per output file**, each carrying:

- `file` — the output filename (make it meaningful and sortable, e.g. `Wine - Cabernet 2021.ai`).
- `values` — a `key: value` for each mapped field. Text values include their unit if the frame
  shows one (`"13.5%"`, `"750 mL"`). Image values are absolute file paths.

A spreadsheet maps cleanly: one row → one record, columns → `values` keys (matched to the
profile). For spec-sheet PDFs or loose lists, extract into the same record shape and **show the
user the assembled table before building**.

## Choosing the method per field (quick guide)

| The field is… | Method |
|---|---|
| a number, price, %, or single word in a single-font frame | `sub` |
| a value sitting next to a bold label in the SAME frame | `sub` (swap only the value text) |
| a body after a bold prefix ("INGREDIENTS:", "Contains:") | `bodyAfter` |
| a whole single-font line replaced outright | `contents` |
| a per-record photo / logo / signature (named linked image) | `image` |
| boilerplate to strip once from the template | `removeSub` (as a master fix) |

## When it's NOT the right tool

- The layout itself changes per record (different number of elements, repositioned objects) —
  that's design work, not a merge.
- The variable image is embedded, not linked, or has no name — name/link it once first.
- You need true text reflow that resizes/moves frames — the engine swaps content in place; if a
  longer string overflows a fixed frame, that's a template/layout fix, flagged on the render.

name: scripts/datamerge.jsx
scripts/report.js
scripts/run.sh
content:
#!/bin/zsh
# Illustrator Data Merge runner: execute the engine inside Illustrator with a long Apple-event
# timeout (macOS otherwise caps a do javascript event at ~120s and aborts long batches),
# then generate + open the HTML report. Job is read from ~/.datamerge/job.jsxinc.
AM="$HOME/.datamerge"
osascript <<OSA
with timeout of 1800 seconds
  tell application "Adobe Illustrator" to do javascript (read POSIX file "$AM/datamerge.jsx")
end timeout
OSA
node "$AM/report.js" >/dev/null 2>&1
open "$AM/report.html" 2>/dev/null

name: scripts/snapshot.sh
content:
#!/bin/zsh
# Illustrator Data Merge: snapshot a whole folder to the external backup home before any edit.
# Usage: snapshot.sh "<source folder>" ["<label>"]   -> prints destination path
SRC="$1"
LABEL="${2:-$(basename "$SRC")}"
HOME_BK="$HOME/Desktop/Data Merge Backups"
STAMP="$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H%M%S')"
DEST="$HOME_BK/$LABEL - $STAMP"
mkdir -p "$DEST"
rsync -a --exclude '_previews' "$SRC/" "$DEST/"
echo "$DEST"
